name: Barbequed Ribs, Missouri-Style
recipeIngredient: 4 racks of 3/down pork ribs (each weighing three pounds or less)
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------Basting Sauce-----------------------
1. Combine 1 3/4 cups Vinegar, 2 tablespoons Hot Pepper Sauce, 2 tablespoons Sugar, 1 tablespoon Salt, and 1 tablespoon Freshly ground Black Pepper to make the basting sauce.
2. Mix together all ingredients for the barbeque rub using Salt, Sugar, Ground Cumin, Freshly ground Black Pepper, Chili Powder, and Paprika.
3. Rub ribs thoroughly with the barbeque rub mixture.
4. Place ribs on baking sheets and cook in a 180-degree oven for 3 hours. Do not turn; slow cooking infuses spices.
5. Remove from oven. (Ribs may be covered and refrigerated up to 2 days before grilling)
6. Use a very low charcoal fire with the rack set as high as possible. Grill ribs for 5 minutes to 30 minutes per side, depending on heat and temperature of ribs. Ribs should have a light outer crust and be heated throughout.
7. If you prefer juicy ribs, coat with basting sauce just before removing from grill. Otherwise serve dry with sauce on the side. Remove ribs from grill, cut between bones, and serve.
